title: Trump Economic Approval Hits Career Low of 31%; Overall Rating Remains Near Historic Lows
detail: New polling released around April 17-18, 2026 shows Trump's approval rating on the economy fell to a career low of 31% (CNN), with economic net approval at -22 and inflation approval at -34. His overall approval rating stands at 35% in CNN polling (one point off his all-time low). Quinnipiac showed 38% approving and 56% disapproving. The Silver Bulletin aggregate put Trump's net approval at -16.6, approximately unchanged from the -16.3 recorded when the Iran ceasefire began. However, his approval among Republicans improved to 81%, up from 72% in March, suggesting the GOP base consolidation continues even as the broader electorate grows more disapproving of his handling of the Iran conflict and the economy.

media.caption: Trump approval ratings: economy at career-low 31%, overall net -16.6

title: Brent Crude Falls 10%+ as Iran Opens Hormuz — Trump's Conditional Blockade Adds Uncertainty
detail: Global oil prices plunged more than 10% over April 17-18, 2026 following Iran's declaration that the Strait of Hormuz is 'completely open' to commercial shipping. The drop erased much of the spike triggered by Trump's April 12 blockade announcement. However, Trump's statement that the US naval blockade 'will remain in full force' until a comprehensive peace deal, combined with Iran's characterization of the re-opening as subject to 'strict management and control,' introduced uncertainty about whether the Hormuz supply disruption is truly resolved. Markets are watching the April 22 ceasefire renewal deadline as a critical inflection point. Hormuz carries 20-21% of global daily oil supply.

media.caption: Oil prices drop 10%+ as Iran declares Strait of Hormuz open; US blockade still active
